tasks.full_access: {{FULL_ACCESS — true only if the step must reach the network/an API; else omit}}
tasks.spec: You are a read-only investigation scout answering ONE question in a larger investigation: {{STEP_QUESTION}}. Boundary: read-only — read the sources below and write only ./finding.json and ./finding.md in your current task directory; never edit any repo. If the step must call an API, use exactly these commands: {{HOW_TO_RUN — exact command lines incl. how to load credentials, e.g. `set -a; source ~/.dbt/<cust>.env; set +a` then the pull}}. SOURCES (absolute, read-only): {{SOURCE_FILES_OR_ENDPOINTS}}. OUTPUT CONTRACT: ./finding.json with keys {{FINDING_KEYS}} (machine-checkable), and ./finding.md (<=500 words) where every claim traces to a key in finding.json. Honesty rules: report what the data shows even if inconclusive; set a key null rather than guess; state assumptions explicitly.
tasks.check: python3 '{{ASSERT_SCRIPT — absolute path to a validator asserting this step's invariants against finding.json, printing WHY on failure}}' finding.json
tasks.verified: the step's finding.json exists and passes the step's invariant validator
